themes.label: Peace/Rest
themes.summary: This chapter offers peace, rest, stillness, comfort, or settled safety in God. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 6 verses. Commentary leans on "Post-Exilic Context: The Chronicler wrote during the Persian period (450-400 BCE) to a commun...". Word-study detail reinforces "מִמְּנ֖וֹחַ: had rest - quiet, i.e., (concretely) a settled spot, or (figuratively) a home".

themes.label: Holiness
themes.summary: This chapter focuses on holiness, righteousness, purity, consecration, or being set apart. Local Strong’s word study and cross references data reinforce this reading across 1 verse. Commentary leans on "But Aaron and his sons (וְאַהֲרֹן וּבָנָיו)—this emphatic contrast distinguishes the exclusiv...". Cross references echo "Holy".
